name: sql_instructions_silver_soc2
description: SQL execution agent instructions/examples for building and validating SOC2 silver-layer assets, monitoring evidence, vulnerability evidence, and risk snapshots.

items.question: Which Snyk-derived assets were seen in the last 24 hours?
items.instructions: Identify active, recently observed software assets to confirm scan coverage and freshness.
items.sql:
SELECT a.nuid, a.dev_id, a.final_name, a.device_subtype, a.device_zone,
       GREATEST(a.events_timestamp, a.last_processed, a.store_updated_at) AS last_seen_ts
FROM public.dev_assets a
WHERE a.device_type = 'software_asset'
  AND a.is_retired = FALSE
  AND GREATEST(a.events_timestamp, a.last_processed, a.store_updated_at) >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours')
ORDER BY last_seen_ts DESC;

items.question: Which in-scope SOC2 assets have stale Snyk scanner check-ins?
items.instructions: Find assets where Snyk agent telemetry is stale, indicating a monitoring control evidence gap.
items.sql:
SELECT a.nuid, a.dev_id, a.final_name,
       ag.agent_last_checkin_ts,
       EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - ag.agent_last_checkin_ts))/60.0 AS minutes_since_checkin,
       ag.agent_health_state
FROM public.dev_assets a
JOIN public.dev_agents ag
  ON a.nuid = ag.nuid AND a.dev_id = ag.dev_id
WHERE ag.agent_type = 'snyk_scanner'
  AND a.is_retired = FALSE
  AND (ag.agent_last_checkin_ts IS NULL OR ag.agent_last_checkin_ts < (NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'))
ORDER BY minutes_since_checkin DESC NULLS LAST;

items.question: Which assets have an open KEV vulnerability (highest urgency)?
items.instructions: List assets with at least one open KEV vulnerability to prioritize immediate remediation.
items.sql:
SELECT v.nuid, v.dev_id, a.final_name, v.cve,
       v.cvss_score, v.status, v.first_seen_at, v.last_seen_at
FROM public.dev_vulnerability_instances v
JOIN public.dev_assets a
  ON a.nuid = v.nuid AND a.dev_id = v.dev_id
WHERE v.is_kev = TRUE
  AND v.status IN ('open','active','unresolved')
ORDER BY v.cvss_score DESC NULLS LAST, v.last_seen_at DESC;
items.context: SOC2 likelihood escalator based on KEV evidence.

items.question: Show SOC2 per-asset risk with primary driver and risk level (last snapshot).
items.instructions: Return SOC2 impact/likelihood/risk and explainability fields to drive triage queues.
items.sql:
SELECT s.snapshot_ts, s.nuid, s.dev_id, a.final_name,
       s.soc2_raw_impact, s.soc2_raw_likelihood, s.soc2_raw_risk,
       s.soc2_impact_class, s.soc2_likelihood_class, s.soc2_risk_level,
       s.soc2_risk_driver_primary
FROM public.soc2_asset_risk_timeseries s
JOIN public.dev_assets a
  ON a.nuid = s.nuid AND a.dev_id = s.dev_id
WHERE s.snapshot_ts = (SELECT MAX(snapshot_ts) FROM public.soc2_asset_risk_timeseries)
ORDER BY s.soc2_raw_risk DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 200;

items.question: Which assets fail the SOC2 monitoring control due to missing or stale Snyk agent evidence?
items.instructions: Evaluate monitoring control state based on agent presence and freshness. Output pass/fail/unknown candidates.
items.sql:
SELECT a.nuid, a.dev_id, a.final_name,
       CASE
         WHEN ag.nuid IS NULL THEN 'fail'
         WHEN ag.agent_last_checkin_ts IS NULL THEN 'unknown'
         WHEN ag.agent_last_checkin_ts < (NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours') THEN 'fail'
         ELSE 'pass'
       END AS control_state,
       ag.agent_last_checkin_ts,
       ag.agent_health_state
FROM public.dev_assets a
LEFT JOIN public.dev_agents ag
  ON a.nuid = ag.nuid AND a.dev_id = ag.dev_id AND ag.agent_type = 'snyk_scanner'
WHERE a.is_retired = FALSE
ORDER BY control_state, ag.agent_last_checkin_ts NULLS FIRST;
items.context: SOC2 control evaluation candidate query (monitoring coverage).
